channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Two jurors claim they were bullied into convicting Harvey Weinstein and regret it, his lawyers say
item.description: Two jurors who voted in June to convict Harvey Weinstein of sexual assault said they regret the decision and only did so because others on the panel bullied them, the former movie mogul’s lawyers said in a newly public court filing.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's quest for the Nobel Peace Prize falls short again despite high-profile nominations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump was passed over for the Nobel Peace Prize on Friday despite jockeying from his fellow Republicans, various world leaders and — most vocally — himself.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meet your favorite streamers at TwitchCon San Diego 2025
item.description: Video game streamers will take over the San Diego Convention Center for a weekend of meet and greets, interactive workshops, live esports and more at TwitchCon San Diego 2025, set for Oct. 17-19.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: 22 states are in a recession or on the brink: Moody’s
item.description: It’s more sobering news for the economy as last week U.S. hiring plans fell to their lowest level since 2009.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, Fox 5

item.title: Cash App giving $100 to users through October. How to enter
item.description: CashApp is giving away $100 to thousands of users every Friday through Oct. 24, as part of its Cash App Pool sweepstakes.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What we know about the fraud case against New York attorney general Letitia James
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Attorney General Letitia James, a longtime political foe of President Donald Trump, was indicted by a federal grand jury Thursday on mortgage fraud charges.
item.dc:creator: BY PHILIP MARCELO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: AstraZeneca agrees to lower drug prices for Medicaid under Trump administration deal
item.description: AstraZeneca on Friday became the second major pharmaceutical manufacturer to announce it had agreed to lower the cost of prescription drugs for Medicaid under a deal struck with the Trump administration that avoided its threats of steep tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Green group files Hatch Act complaints against HHS, Forest Service over 'partisan' shutdown messaging
item.description: An environmental advocacy group has filed complaints under the Hatch Act against the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) and the Forest Service over what it described as “partisan” messaging blaming Democrats for the ongoing government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Did Trump end 7 wars? What to know ahead of Nobel Peace Prize announcement
item.description: President Trump's moment of truth as a global peacemaker will come early Friday morning when the Nobel Peace Prize is awarded. Trump has made no secret of his desire to win the prize.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHS blames Democrats for shutdown in video to be shown at TSA checkpoints
item.description: A representative for the DHS provided a statement confirming that Kristi Noem’s remarks would be shown “in airports across the country.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Oklahoma's GOP governor objects to Texas troops in Illinois
item.description: “Oklahomans would lose their mind if Pritzker in Illinois sent troops down to Oklahoma during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: MIT president says she 'cannot support' proposal to adopt Trump priorities for funding benefits
item.description: MIT's president said she “cannot support” a White House proposal that asks MIT and eight other universities to adopt President Donald Trump’s political agenda in exchange for favorable access to federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Blast at a Tennessee explosives plant leaves 18 missing and feared dead, sheriff says
item.description: Officials say an explosion at a Tennessee explosives plant has left 18 people missing and feared dead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump does not win Nobel Peace Prize despite Israel-Hamas deal and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope urges news agencies to stand as bulwark against lies, manipulation and post-truths
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Leo XIV encouraged international news agencies on Thursday to stand firm as a bulwark against the “ancient art of lying” and manipulation, as he strongly backed a free, independent and objective press.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: A history of President Trump's legal battles with New York Attorney General Letitia James
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The day she was elected New York's attorney general, Letitia James blasted President Donald Trump as a “con man” and “carnival barker" and pledged to scrutinize his public policies and personal business dealings.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tennessee teacher fired over Charlie Kirk posts files free speech lawsuit
item.description: A longtime Rutherford County teacher is suing her school district, claiming she was fired for what she shared online.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Milan, KRON4

item.title: Political Power Ranker: Analyzing this year's biggest elections
item.description: On this week's edition of Political Power Ranker, we're breaking down some of the biggest races across the country.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KRON4

item.title: Harvard brass mum as rival schools denounce political violence after series of professor blunders
item.description: Harvard University refuses to condemn political violence after hosting a controversial 2018 panel where faculty discussed armed political action.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gene Simmons admits to being a 'horrible driver' after fainting, crashing into parked car
item.description: KISS rocker Gene Simmons is thanking fans for their concern after he crashed his SUV earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WGN 9

item.title: Palisades Fire suspect held without bond
item.description: Jonathan Rinderknecht, 29, accused of using open flame to start fire that became Palisades blaze, denied bail due to mental health and family issues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Small businesses feeling pinch of government shutdown
item.description: When the government shuts down, the Small Business Administration stops processing new loans and approving routine small-business loans.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Nieves, KRON4

item.title: World leaders express hope after Trump says Israel and Hamas agreed to first phase of peace deal
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — World leaders on Thursday expressed hope for peace and urged Israel and Hamas to fulfill their commitments in the hours after U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jail's tight leash on Charlie Kirk's alleged assassin all about 'safety,' not silencing, expert says
item.description: Former federal prison official explains why the Utah jail isolating alleged Charlie Kirk assassin Tyler Robinson is acting out of safety, not punishment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nobel Prize in literature awarded to Hungarian writer László Krasznahorkai
item.description: Hungarian writer László Krasznahorkai, whose philosophical, bleakly funny novels often unfold in single sentences, won the Nobel Prize in literature Thursday for his for his "compelling and visionary oeuvre that, in the midst of apocalyptic terror, reaffirms the power of art.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV and MIKE CORDER Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: US diplomat fired for romantic relationship with woman with ties to Chinese Communist Party
item.description: The State Department said it fired a U.S. diplomat who concealed his relationship with a woman who had known ties to the Chinese Communist Party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel and Hamas agree to part of Trump's Gaza peace plan, will free hostages and prisoners
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Israel and Hamas agreed Wednesday to pause fighting in Gaza so that the remaining hostages there can be freed in the coming days in exchange for Palestinian prisoners, accepting elements of a plan put forward by the Trump administration that would represent the biggest breakthrough
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, SAM MEDNICK and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Prosecutors say no harm was done by social media posts about assassination of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal prosecutors told a judge Wednesday that no harm was done to prospects for a fair trial after two U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More than 54,600 children younger than 5 may be acutely malnourished in Gaza, study finds
item.description: After two years of war and dire food shortages, more than 54,600 children younger than 5 in Gaza may be acutely malnourished, with more than 12,800 severely affected, according to a new study by a U.N. agency.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Woman sues haunted house after breaking both ankles
item.description: A Nebraska woman says a jump meant to thrill visitors at a haunted house in Kansas City, Missouri, left her with two broken ankles.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: Lawsuit blames Las Vegas company in 'Hawaii Five-0' actor's death
item.description: A wrongful death lawsuit filed by the heirs of an actor famed for his "Hawaii Five-0" role claims that a Las Vegas-based company is responsible for the injuries that eventually killed him.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pro-Palestinian protesters arrested in Boston after clash with police
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Thirteen pro-Palestinian protesters were arrested in Boston after a chaotic clash with police that resulted in four officers being sent to area hospitals for non-life-threatening injuries, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: LEAH WILLINGHAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The shutdown is already causing flight delays. Service to more than 170 airports could stop entirely
item.description: Several major airports were experiencing delays on Wednesday, and smaller airports were at risk of losing service altogether by the end of the week.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Nobel Prize in chemistry goes to discovery that could trap C02 and bring water to deserts
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Three scientists won the Nobel Prize in chemistry Wednesday for their development of new molecular structures that can trap vast quantities of gas inside, laying the groundwork to potentially suck greenhouse gases out of the atmosphere or harvest moisture from desert environments.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV, STEFANIE DAZIO and CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Robert Roberson is again approaching execution in Texas in shaken baby case
item.description: LIVINGSTON, Texas (AP) — Robert Roberson was calm and hopeful as he pondered his mortality and whether he could again avoid becoming the first person in the U.S. executed for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome. With days to go before his scheduled Oct.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bob Ross paintings to be auctioned to support public TV stations after federal funding cuts
item.description: Thirty paintings created by the bushy-haired, soft-spoken Bob Ross will soon be up for auction to defray the costs of programming for small and rural public television stations suffering under cuts in federal funding.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Groups sue EPA over canceled $7 billion solar program intended to help poorer Americans
item.description: Several groups and nonprofit organizations filed a lawsuit Monday against the Environmental Protection Agency over the canceling of a $7 billion Solar for All program intended to make solar power accessible to more than 900,000 lower-income Americans.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4
